steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers found that great blue herons prefer deep water during breeding season, but small dark herons do not care about water depth. The conclusion must logically follow from this asymmetry: water-depth management strategies should affect the two species differently.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'attract great blue herons...than...attract small dark herons': water-depth strategies would more effectively target great blue herons (depth-sensitive) than small dark herons (depth-insensitive). Follows directly from the finding. ✓
  • Choice B — 'decrease the area's appeal to both': contradicts the purpose of water management and the finding about great blue herons preferring deep water. ✗
  • Choice C — 'extend the average lifespan of great blue herons': the study is about habitat selection, not lifespan. Introduces an out-of-scope claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'attract birds that don't typically live in wetlands': the study only concerns these two wetland species; non-wetland birds are never discussed. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
